_doc.what: Annotated sample for .bee/config.json — copy the sections you need; every key here is optional (missing keys fall back to safe defaults). JSON has no comments, so this _doc block is the documentation; bee ignores unknown top-level keys, but remove _doc from your real config to keep it lean.
_doc.hooks: Per-hook kill switches. All nine ship enabled; set one to false only to debug that hook. Names match the hook files: session-init, prompt-context, write-guard, model-guard, state-sync, chain-nudge, session-close, tools-logger, codex-subagent-audit.
_doc.guards: idle_gate=true blocks source edits when no bee work is active (phase idle / compounding-complete). Disable per-repo only if you accept unrouted edits: bee config set --key guards.idle_gate --value false. max_read_lines (default 800) caps how many lines a single inbound file read may pull before the read guard trims it.
_doc.commands: The host project's own lifecycle commands — record real runnable commands, not descriptions. Two-tier test doctrine (ci-owned-verify): `test` is the SCOPED dev-loop command — only the tests related to the current change (use your runner's changed-only/related mode); it powers the orchestrator's wave-close check and the `bee worktree merge` semantic gate. `verify` is the FULL suite — CI-owned: it runs on push and at release, never as a local per-cell or session-start obligation; a red CI run auto-files a `verify-red` issue. Any consumer missing `test` falls back to `verify`. Per-language recipes (node/go/rust/python/php): docs/config-reference.md.

gate_bypass.values: false | "normal" | "full" | "total" (legacy true = normal). Set via the bee-bypass-gate skill, not by hand.
gate_bypass.false: default — every gate stops for the human.
gate_bypass.normal: auto-approves Gates 1-3 for tiny/small/standard work; high-risk/hard-gate work, secret reads, and review-P1 still stop.
gate_bypass.full: also auto-approves high-risk/hard-gate Gates 1-3; only secret reads and a review P1 still stop.
gate_bypass.total: zero stops: every gate, secret reads, and review P1 findings auto-proceed. The loud session banner reminds you it is on.

models.what: Only the CHEAPER tiers are configured, keyed by runtime (claude | codex) then slot (extraction | generation | review | advisor). The ceiling is never configured — it is always the session model. A missing/null slot = prompt-budget tier (the marker alone carries the tier; the subagent inherits the session model).

slot_shapes.string: "haiku" — plain model name; dispatches carry it as the model param (economics: effective_model_status=pinned on claude).
slot_shapes.object_cli: { "kind": "cli", "command": "...", "promptVia": "stdin" } — external CLI executor. GATHER-ONLY by design: cell execution against a cli-shaped slot is refused (cli_tier_gather_only); gathers/reviewers/advisors run through it with the prompt piped via stdin. See config-sample-cli-executors.json for full examples.
slot_shapes.null: slot unset — prompt-budget tier; on codex-native transport the effective model is inherited-or-unknown (the dispatch log records the honest split).

models.advisor: The AO3/AO13 Gate-3 consult identity for high-risk work. Claude default: fable (the session-class model). Set null to skip the advisor line in worker prompts when no advisor resolves.
models.codex_note: On codex-cli 0.144.4 native subagents cannot pin a per-agent model — a string here records the REQUESTED model; the dispatch log will still say effective_model_status=inherited-or-unknown until a capability probe proves per-agent selection.

_doc.lanes: Reserved per-repo lane config overrides; leave {} unless a bee release documents a key.
_doc.capabilities: Reserved capability flags; leave {} unless a bee release documents a key.
_doc.dogfood_repos: Only used in bee's own source repo (self-improvement loops); host projects can omit or leave [].
_doc.product_root: Optional, for the workshop+nested-product topology only: where the product's own docs live when they are NOT beside .bee/ (path relative to the bee root, or absolute). Unset = the bee root; do not set it speculatively — a set-but-missing path warns loudly on every read.
